title: ulmus campestris f. laevis
intro: Ulmus campestris f. laevis, also known as the American white elm or white elm, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its broad leaves and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Ulmus campestris f. laevis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Ulmus campestris f. laevis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the form of bark or leaf extracts, to harness its medicinal properties.
